meta.title: MS-FAE-5: Lakehouse Delivery, Direct Lake, and Semantic Control

domains.name: SQL Analytics Endpoint and Lakehouse SQL

questions.stem: A semantic model is difficult for analysts because sales and returns are blended into one wide table with repeating attributes. What redesign is most appropriate?

options.text: Store all logic only in report text boxes
Refactor toward a star schema with conformed dimensions and fact tables
Move the table to a slide deck

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because A star schema usually improves maintainability, performance, and user comprehension.

questions.stem: An engineer needs the most appropriate approach for this requirement. why are dimension tables typically related one-to-many to fact tables in a star schema?

options.text: So filters can propagate from descriptive attributes to transactional facts efficiently
Because facts should never contain numeric values
Because dimensions must always be larger than facts

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Star schemas use dimension-to-fact relationships to support intuitive slicing and efficient analytics.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Fabric capability orchestrates activities such as copy operations, notebook runs, and data movement steps?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Fabric data pipelines orchestrate multi-step data movement and processing activities.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which statement best describes the difference between a Fabric Lakehouse and a Fabric Warehouse?

options.text: A warehouse can store only CSV files
A lakehouse is Spark-first over Delta tables, while a warehouse provides a SQL warehousing experience
A warehouse cannot use OneLake storage
A lakehouse cannot expose SQL access

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Lakehouses are oriented to Spark and open table files, while warehouses provide a SQL-focused warehousing experience.

questions.stem: In this scenario, what is the main role of DAX in Fabric semantic models?

options.text: Define calculations such as measures, calculated columns, and tables

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because DAX is the calculation language used in tabular semantic models.

questions.stem: An engineer needs the most appropriate approach for this requirement. why can a good star schema improve report performance?

options.text: It forces all visuals to use the same color
It reduces ambiguity and often creates more efficient filter and aggregation paths
It guarantees no refresh is ever needed

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Well-structured dimensional models usually perform and scale better than messy wide-table designs.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about OneLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It requires each workload to provision a separate physical lake per item.
It is only available to Power BI dashboards and nothing else.
It is the unified storage foundation for Fabric data items.
Shortcuts can reference data in other storage systems.

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **C and D** because OneLake is Fabric’s unified lake and supports shortcuts to external data sources.

questions.stem: A team is validating its design choice. why might a Fabric team use endorsements such as promoted or certified?

options.text: To signal trusted, recommended data assets to consumers

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Endorsements help users discover and trust the right artifacts.

questions.stem: During a production readiness review, the team asks: why might a model author create measures instead of many static pre-aggregated columns?

options.text: Measures are required for OneLake shortcuts
Measures compute reusable business logic at query time in a controlled model

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Measures centralize business logic and support flexible aggregations across filter contexts.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which schema style is generally recommended for Power BI semantic models because it simplifies filtering and relationships?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because A star schema is the common recommended design for analytical semantic models.

questions.stem: A nightly process must wait for upstream ingestion to finish before refreshing downstream models. What is the best approach?

options.text: Orchestrate the dependency in a Fabric pipeline
Only notify users by email and hope timing works

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Pipelines are the orchestration layer for dependency-aware refresh and processing steps.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO tasks are common uses of Fabric deployment pipelines? (Select TWO)

options.text: Replace semantic models with raw image files
Promote items across development, test, and production stages
Manage controlled release of BI and data artifacts

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **C and D** because Deployment pipelines support structured promotion of Fabric items across environments.

questions.stem: A team wants to minimize duplicate semantic definitions and keep curated SQL-facing objects close to governed storage. What is the best direction?

options.text: Model over curated Fabric lakehouse or warehouse objects instead of rebuilding logic in many disconnected files
Use only manually exported CSV files

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Keeping curated SQL-facing objects in Fabric reduces duplication and improves governance.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO practices usually improve report consumption experiences? (Select TWO)

options.text: Design visuals around clear business questions
Expose every technical field by default regardless of audience
Use a governed semantic model with meaningful measures

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **B and D** because Governed models and audience-focused design improve report clarity and trust.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about SQL access in Fabric are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Dataflows Gen2 are the primary engine for DAX measures.
Warehouses provide a SQL-first analytical experience.
Spark notebooks require no workspace to run.
Lakehouses expose a SQL analytics endpoint for querying tables.

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **B and D** because Fabric supports SQL through both lakehouse endpoints and warehouses, but they serve different experiences.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about DirectLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It disables semantic modeling features entirely.
It is designed to query large semantic models over OneLake data with minimal import latency.
It aims to reduce the need to copy data into a traditional import cache first.
It requires every report to run only against Excel files.

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **B and C** because DirectLake is intended to query OneLake-backed data efficiently without the same ingestion pattern as classic import.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO actions commonly help analytical performance in Fabric BI solutions? (Select TWO)

options.text: Monitor capacity and workload behavior over time
Design efficient semantic models and relationships

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **C and D** because Performance depends on sound modeling and ongoing monitoring of workload behavior.

questions.stem: A DirectLake model is attractive to a team because it reduces which common bottleneck?

options.text: The need for any semantic model at all
The need to repeatedly import data into a separate in-memory cache before analysis

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because DirectLake aims to reduce import latency by reading more directly from OneLake-backed data.

questions.stem: In this scenario, when is a Fabric Warehouse typically a stronger choice than a Lakehouse?

options.text: When users only need notebook markdown cells
When the workload is primarily SQL-centric warehousing and dimensional modeling
When there is no tabular data
When Power BI must be disabled

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Warehouses are optimized for SQL warehousing experiences.

questions.stem: A copy activity occasionally fails due to transient network issues. Which pipeline configuration most directly helps?
questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Retries on the activity help recover from transient failures without manual reruns.

questions.stem: During a production readiness review, the team asks: why are field parameters useful in reports?

options.text: They let users switch displayed dimensions or measures dynamically in visuals

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Field parameters improve report interactivity by letting users change what a visual displays.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Fabric item primarily uses Power Query for data shaping and transformation?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 use Power Query-based transformation patterns.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Fabric-adjacent experience is primarily used to build interactive BI reports and dashboards for end users?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Power BI is the reporting and dashboard experience consumed with Fabric data assets.

questions.stem: A solution needs notebook-based engineering with Delta tables plus downstream BI over the same governed data. Which Fabric design is most aligned?

options.text: Use a lakehouse in OneLake and build semantic models over curated tables
Keep source files only in local desktops
Use paginated reports as the storage layer

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because A lakehouse supports engineering on Delta tables and can feed governed BI models from the same platform foundation.

questions.stem: A report is popular but users complain that each page repeats slightly different revenue logic. What is the best corrective action?

options.text: Export to CSV for each consumer
Hardcode separate visual-level formulas on every page forever
Centralize the revenue definition in the semantic model

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Centralizing business logic in the semantic model improves consistency across reports.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Fabric capability helps admins monitor capacity utilization and performance trends?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because The Capacity Metrics app helps monitor workload and capacity usage.

questions.stem: In this scenario, what is the primary use of a lakehouse SQL analytics endpoint?

options.text: Read-only T-SQL querying over lakehouse tables

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because The SQL analytics endpoint provides SQL querying over lakehouse data.

questions.stem: A team wants to expose data that remains in Amazon S3 without copying it into OneLake first. Which Fabric feature should it use?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because OneLake shortcuts let Fabric reference data stored in external locations without full physical duplication in OneLake.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which item automatically includes a SQL analytics endpoint for querying lakehouse tables?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because A Fabric Lakehouse includes a SQL analytics endpoint for read-oriented SQL access to lakehouse tables.

questions.stem: A team wants reusable transformation logic with environment promotion and fewer manual production changes. Which combination is strongest?

options.text: Use report bookmarks for ETL scheduling
Store production logic in chat messages
Use Dataflows Gen2 or notebooks for transforms and deployment pipelines for promotion
Rely only on ad hoc desktop edits

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Reusable transform assets plus deployment pipelines support repeatable delivery.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which feature restricts which rows a user can see in a semantic model based on identity or assigned role?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Row-level security filters model data for different user audiences.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO characteristics are desirable in production-grade Fabric data pipelines? (Select TWO)

options.text: Manual recreation of every activity in each workspace

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **A and B** because Observability and parameterization are core traits of reliable pipelines.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which scenario best fits Dataflows Gen2?

options.text: Low-code ingestion and shaping of data with Power Query

questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 are designed for low-code data ingestion and transformation.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO practices generally improve semantic model usability? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create ambiguous duplicate relationships whenever possible
Model around well-defined dimensions and facts
Use clear business-friendly names and measures
Hide all fields regardless of purpose

questions.explanation: The correct answers are **B and C** because Clear naming and sound dimensional design improve usability and correctness.

questions.stem: In this scenario, what does a semantic model in Power BI or Fabric primarily provide?

options.text: A business-friendly analytical layer over underlying data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Semantic models present curated tables, relationships, and calculations for analytics.

questions.stem: A large semantic model should refresh less data after initial historical processing. Which feature is most relevant?
questions.explanation: **C** is correct because Incremental refresh reduces processing by refreshing only recent partitions after the initial load.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Microsoft Fabric capability provides a single logical data lake across Fabric experiences?
questions.explanation: **D** is correct because OneLake is the tenant-wide logical data lake underlying Fabric storage experiences.

questions.stem: A company wants sensitive financial data to be visible only to finance analysts, while the same report shell is used company-wide. Which design is most appropriate?

options.text: Apply row-level security or separate governed model access for audiences

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Governed model access and RLS let different audiences use the same analytical assets safely.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO controls commonly support governed BI delivery in Fabric? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: The correct answers are **A and D** because Access control and RLS are central governance mechanisms for BI assets.

questions.stem: In this scenario, which admin-facing Fabric tool helps identify workload pressure on capacity over time?
questions.explanation: **C** is correct because The Capacity Metrics app helps admins understand utilization and workload behavior over time.
